title: TML EIP-712 Typed Data Schema Bundle
description: EIP-712 domain separator and type definitions for off-chain structured data hashing in the TML framework. Defines typed data for MoralTraceLog and PermissionToken. Distinct from the on-chain ABI (tml_abi.json). Authority: TML Constitutionalization Monograph v3.3, EIP-712 (Ethereum Typed Structured Data Hashing and Signing).
$comment: Monograph ref: Section 2.3.3, Section 5B.i, Section 8. EIP-712 typed data only. No Solidity source. These definitions enable off-chain signing of TML artifacts with on-chain verifiability.

eip712Domain.description: EIP-712 domain separator definition for the TML framework. The domain separator binds all typed data signatures to a specific contract, chain, and monograph version, preventing cross-domain and cross-version replay attacks.
eip712Domain.$comment: EIP-712 Section 4 (Domain Separator). chainId must be set to the target deployment chain at runtime.

EIP712Domain.name: name
EIP712Domain.description: Human-readable name of the signing domain.

EIP712Domain.name: version
EIP712Domain.description: Version of the TML monograph this domain is bound to.

EIP712Domain.name: chainId
EIP712Domain.description: EIP-155 chain ID of the target deployment network.

EIP712Domain.name: verifyingContract
EIP712Domain.description: Address of the TML_Core contract. Binds signatures to the specific deployment.

EIP712Domain.name: salt
EIP712Domain.description: Deployment-specific entropy salt to further differentiate domain separators across deployments.

MoralTraceLog.description: EIP-712 typed data schema for off-chain signing of a Moral Trace Log (TSLF) entry. Enables custodians, auditors, and the Anchoring Lane to produce verifiable signatures over TSLF records without full on-chain storage of every field. The signature is verifiable against the signer's address on any EIP-712 compliant verifier.
MoralTraceLog.$comment: Monograph ref: Section 8 (TSLF forensic schema), Pillar II (AlwaysMemory), Pillar IV (MoralTraceLogs). The typehash is: keccak256('MoralTraceLog(bytes32 logId,int8 currentState,bytes32 decisionId,bytes32 envelopeId,bytes32 justificationObjectHash,bytes32 pillarsCertifiedHash,uint256 committedAt,bytes32 monographVersionHash)').

MoralTraceLog.name: logId
MoralTraceLog.description: Unique identifier for this Moral Trace Log entry (UUID v4 packed as bytes32).

MoralTraceLog.name: currentState
MoralTraceLog.description: Triadic state value. +1 = PROCEED. 0 = SACRED_ZERO. -1 = REFUSE. Encoded as int8 to preserve the signed integer semantics of the TML triadic state space. State 0 is SACRED_ZERO: not null, not error, not timeout.

MoralTraceLog.name: decisionId
MoralTraceLog.description: Identifier of the decision that produced this log entry.

MoralTraceLog.name: envelopeId
MoralTraceLog.description: Identifier of the State Envelope associated with this log entry.

MoralTraceLog.name: justificationObjectHash
MoralTraceLog.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON serialization of the JustificationObject that traveled between Inference and Anchoring lanes.

MoralTraceLog.name: pillarsCertifiedHash
MoralTraceLog.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical serialization of the pillarsCertified array. Commits the set of Eight Pillars assessed at log creation without expanding them into the typed struct.

MoralTraceLog.name: committedAt
MoralTraceLog.description: Unix epoch timestamp of pre-actuation commit (AlwaysMemory). Committed before any human review or Permission Token issuance.

MoralTraceLog.name: monographVersionHash
MoralTraceLog.description: keccak256 hash of the monograph version string '3.3.0-tml-monograph-2025'. Binds this log to the specific monograph version under which it was produced.

signingInstructions.description: Instructions for producing a valid EIP-712 signature over a MoralTraceLog typed data instance.
steps: 1. Construct the MoralTraceLog struct with all required fields populated per the TSLF schema (tml_schema.json).
2. Compute justificationObjectHash as SHA-256 of the canonical JSON serialization of the JustificationObject.
3. Compute pillarsCertifiedHash as SHA-256 of the canonical JSON serialization of the pillarsCertified array.
5. Encode the struct per EIP-712 typeHash and hashStruct rules.
6. Produce the final digest: keccak256(abi.encodePacked('\x19\x01', domainSeparator, structHash)).
7. Sign the digest with the HSM-resident key identified by SignatureBlock.signerKeyId.
8. Record the signature in the Goukassian Promise SignatureBlock artifact.

example.description: Illustrative field values for a State 0 (SacredZero) MoralTraceLog typed data instance. All values are illustrative only.
example.$comment: currentState 0 = SACRED_ZERO. The typed data encoding preserves the int8 signed value, ensuring 0 is not conflated with false, null, or an error code.

PermissionToken.description: EIP-712 typed data schema for off-chain signing of a Permission Token by the Anchoring Lane. The signed PermissionToken is the cryptographic release key for the actuation layer. Its off-chain signature enables the actuation layer to verify authorization without a gas-bearing on-chain call for every action, while the logHash and merkleRoot fields maintain verifiability against the on-chain TML_Core anchor record. This is the off-chain expression of No Log = No Action.
PermissionToken.$comment: Monograph ref: Section 2.3.3, Section 5B.i. The typehash is: keccak256('PermissionToken(bytes32 tokenId,bytes32 logHash,uint256 epochTimestamp,bytes32 signerKeyId,bytes32 merkleRoot,uint256 expiresAt,bytes32 decisionId,bytes32 monographVersionHash)').

PermissionToken.name: tokenId
PermissionToken.description: Unique identifier for this Permission Token (UUID v4 packed as bytes32).

PermissionToken.name: logHash
PermissionToken.description: SHA-256 hash of the anchored Moral Trace Log that authorizes this token. Core binding field: no Permission Token is valid without a corresponding anchored log. This is the typed-data expression of No Log = No Action.

PermissionToken.name: epochTimestamp
PermissionToken.description: Unix epoch timestamp at which the Anchoring Lane issued this token.

PermissionToken.name: signerKeyId
PermissionToken.description: keccak256 hash of the HSM signing key identifier. Identifies the Anchoring Lane key that produced this token's signature.

PermissionToken.name: merkleRoot
PermissionToken.description: SHA-256 Merkle root of the batch in which the authorizing log is anchored. Non-repudiation binding: must match the root in the TML_Core on-chain anchor record.

PermissionToken.name: expiresAt
PermissionToken.description: Unix epoch timestamp after which this token is invalid for actuation authorization. The actuation layer MUST reject tokens where block.timestamp >= expiresAt.

PermissionToken.name: decisionId
PermissionToken.description: Identifier of the decision for which this token was issued. Prevents token reuse across decisions.

PermissionToken.name: monographVersionHash
PermissionToken.description: keccak256 hash of the monograph version string '3.3.0-tml-monograph-2025'. Binds this token to the specific monograph version.

securityProperties.description: Security properties enforced by the EIP-712 PermissionToken typed data schema.
securityProperties.noLogNoAction: logHash binds the token to a specific anchored Moral Trace Log. The actuation layer must verify that logHash is included in the merkleRoot before accepting the token.
securityProperties.anchoringLaneOnly: The token is signed by the HSM key identified by signerKeyId, which must be a key registered to the Anchoring Lane in the HybridShield custodian registry. Inference Lane keys are not authorized to sign Permission Tokens.
securityProperties.expiryEnforcement: The actuation layer must reject any PermissionToken where the current timestamp is at or after expiresAt. Expiry is a hard constraint, not a warning.
securityProperties.domainBinding: The EIP-712 domain separator binds this token to a specific chainId, verifyingContract, and monograph version. Cross-chain and cross-deployment token reuse is cryptographically prevented.
securityProperties.decisionBinding: The decisionId field prevents a valid Permission Token from being replayed against a different decision. One token authorizes one decision.
securityProperties.merkleVerifiability: The merkleRoot field enables any party to verify the token's authorization chain: token -> logHash -> merkleRoot -> on-chain TML_Core anchor record -> public blockchain transaction.

signingInstructions.description: Instructions for producing a valid EIP-712 signature over a PermissionToken typed data instance.
steps: 1. Confirm the authorizing Moral Trace Log is anchored: logHash must be verifiable against an anchored merkleRoot in TML_Core.
2. Construct the PermissionToken struct with all required fields.
5. Encode the struct per EIP-712 typeHash and hashStruct rules.
6. Produce the final digest: keccak256(abi.encodePacked('\x19\x01', domainSeparator, structHash)).
7. Sign the digest with the Anchoring Lane HSM key identified by signerKeyId.
8. Record the signatureValue in the PermissionToken JSON schema object (tml_schema.json#/$defs/PermissionToken.signatureValue) as a base64url-encoded value.
9. The actuation layer verifies the signature against the public key before authorizing execution.

example.description: Illustrative field values for a PermissionToken typed data instance for a State +1 (PROCEED) decision. All values are illustrative only.
example.$comment: The actuation layer: (1) verifies EIP-712 signature against signerKeyId public key, (2) confirms logHash is included in merkleRoot via Merkle inclusion proof, (3) confirms merkleRoot is anchored in TML_Core on-chain, (4) confirms current timestamp < expiresAt, (5) confirms decisionId matches the pending decision. Only then does execution proceed.

EmergencyOverride.description: EIP-712 typed data schema for off-chain signing of Emergency Override authorizations by supreme authority identities. The signed data is submitted to TML_Core.executeEmergencyOverride. Signing this structure constitutes a sovereign authority act; it is immutably logged before execution.
EmergencyOverride.$comment: Monograph ref: Section 13.3 (Emergency Override). x-tml-pillar: SacredZero, AlwaysMemory, HybridShield.

EmergencyOverride.name: overrideRequestId
EmergencyOverride.description: Unique identifier for this override request.

EmergencyOverride.name: targetDecisionId
EmergencyOverride.description: Target decision for FORCED_STATE_TRANSITION. Zero bytes32 for other override types.

EmergencyOverride.name: forcedState
EmergencyOverride.description: Target state for FORCED_STATE_TRANSITION. 0 = REFUSE. 255 = SACRED_ZERO. Forced transition to PROCEED is not permitted.

EmergencyOverride.name: justificationHash
EmergencyOverride.description: SHA-256 hash of the justification document. The justification document is archived off-chain and anchored before the override executes.

EmergencyOverride.name: requestedAt
EmergencyOverride.description: Unix epoch timestamp at which the supreme authority requested this override.

EmergencyOverride.name: monographVersionHash
EmergencyOverride.description: keccak256 hash of '3.3.0-tml-monograph-2025'. Binds the override to the specific monograph under whose authority it was invoked.

CustodianQuorumAttestation.description: EIP-712 typed data schema for custodian signatures in HybridShield quorum operations. Each of the 6 custodians signs this structure to contribute to a quorum for Merkle anchoring, system-wide Sacred Zero activation, or Emergency Override authorization.
CustodianQuorumAttestation.$comment: Monograph ref: Section 2.3.3, Pillar VII (HybridShield). 6-Custodian distributed anchoring.

CustodianQuorumAttestation.name: custodianId
CustodianQuorumAttestation.description: Unique identifier of the attesting custodian node.

CustodianQuorumAttestation.name: operationId
CustodianQuorumAttestation.description: Identifier of the operation (Merkle anchor, Sacred Zero activation, or Emergency Override) this attestation authorizes.

CustodianQuorumAttestation.name: operationPayloadHash
CustodianQuorumAttestation.description: SHA-256 hash of the operation payload (e.g., merkleRoot for MERKLE_ANCHOR, escalationId for SACRED_ZERO_ACTIVATION).

CustodianQuorumAttestation.name: attestedAt
CustodianQuorumAttestation.description: Unix epoch timestamp of custodian attestation.

CustodianQuorumAttestation.name: custodianKeyVersion
CustodianQuorumAttestation.description: Version identifier of the custodian's current key material.

TriCameralApproval.description: EIP-712 typed data schema for off-chain signing of Tri-Cameral governance proposals and votes. Used by Technical Council members (registerTriCameralProposal), Stewardship Custodians (recordTriCameralVote, including binding veto), and Smart Contract Treasury (executeTriCameralRatification). Signing this structure constitutes a constitutional governance act and is immutably logged on-chain. Survivability-class proposals require dual-vote ratification across two 180-day windows. Governance structure is identical to the TL framework by constitutional design.
TriCameralApproval.$comment: Monograph ref: TML Constitutionalization Monograph v3.3, Section III, Section 7A. x-tml-pillar: HybridShield.

TriCameralApproval.name: proposalId
TriCameralApproval.description: Unique identifier for this governance proposal.

TriCameralApproval.name: proposerChamber
TriCameralApproval.description: 0 = TechnicalCouncil (9 members, proposal rights only). Stewardship Custodians hold veto rights only and may not submit proposals.

TriCameralApproval.name: proposerMemberId
TriCameralApproval.description: Identity of the proposing Technical Council member. Chamber membership suspended on submission per Section 7A.

TriCameralApproval.name: proposalHash
TriCameralApproval.description: SHA-256 hash of the full proposal text, anchored off-chain before on-chain registration.

TriCameralApproval.name: survivabilityClass
TriCameralApproval.description: True if this proposal modifies the NL=NA enforcement chain, three-state model, Eight Pillar definitions, chamber composition, quorum thresholds, or any schema const, required, or unevaluatedProperties constraint. Triggers Section 7A dual-vote protocol.

TriCameralApproval.name: clockStartTimestamp
TriCameralApproval.description: Unix epoch timestamp at which the 180-day evaluation window begins. Set at submission regardless of vacancy status. For survivability-class proposals, two consecutive 180-day windows are required.

TriCameralApproval.name: voteNumber
TriCameralApproval.description: 1 = first vote after initial 180-day window. 2 = second vote after second 180-day window. Both must pass at 75% of seatedActiveMembers in both chambers for ratification.

TriCameralApproval.name: vetoExercised
TriCameralApproval.description: Stewardship Custodians only. If true, proposal is constitutionally blocked. vetoExercised=true from any Stewardship Custodian supersedes all vote counts.

TriCameralApproval.name: monographVersionHash
TriCameralApproval.description: keccak256 hash of '3.3.0-tml-monograph-2025'. Binds the governance act to the specific monograph version under whose authority it was executed.

TechnicalCouncilVote.name: votesAbstain
TechnicalCouncilVote.description: Abstentions including vacant seats and quarantined new appointees.

TechnicalCouncilVote.name: seatedActiveMembers
TechnicalCouncilVote.description: Active non-quarantined members at time of vote. Quorum threshold calculated against this value only. Maximum 9.

StewardshipCustodianVote.name: votesAbstain
StewardshipCustodianVote.description: Abstentions including vacant seats and quarantined new appointees.

StewardshipCustodianVote.name: seatedActiveMembers
StewardshipCustodianVote.description: Active non-quarantined members at time of vote. Quorum threshold calculated against this value only. Maximum 11.

StewardshipCustodianVote.name: vetoExercised
StewardshipCustodianVote.description: Binding veto. If true, proposal constitutionally blocked regardless of vote counts. No Technical Council vote can override a Stewardship Custodian veto.

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: EIP-712 typed data schema for the Goukassian Promise Signature artifact. Enables the Goukassian Promise Signature Block to be expressed as an EIP-712 signed structure, binding the TML system's provenance and non-repudiation record to the constitutional chain. 'signature' is the canonical artifact name.
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.$comment: Monograph ref: Section 2.4 (GoukassianPromise: lantern, signature, license). x-tml-pillar: GoukassianPromise.

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: signatureId
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: Unique identifier for this Goukassian Promise Signature attestation.

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: artifactName
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: keccak256 hash of the canonical artifact name string 'signature'. Encodes the canonical Goukassian Promise artifact identity.

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: signerKeyId
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: keccak256 hash of the HSM signing key identifier.

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: signedPayloadHash
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: SHA-256 hash of the payload being attested (log entry, token, or broadcast).

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: signatureAlgorithmId
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: Signature algorithm. 0 = ES256. 1 = ES384. 2 = ES512. 3 = RS256. 4 = RS384. 5 = RS512. 6 = SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s (FUTURE). 7 = ML-KEM-1024 (FUTURE).

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: signedAt
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: Unix epoch timestamp at which the signature was produced.

GoukassianSignatureAttestation.name: monographVersionHash
GoukassianSignatureAttestation.description: keccak256 hash of the monograph version string. Binds this attestation to the specific constitutional version.

typeHashRegistry.description: Pre-computed EIP-712 typeHash strings for each primary type. These are the canonical string representations used to compute keccak256 typeHashes per EIP-712 Section 2.4. Whitespace is significant; these strings must not be reformatted.
typeHashRegistry.$comment: Per EIP-712: typeHash = keccak256(encodeType(primaryType)). These strings are authoritative.
typeHashRegistry.MoralTraceLog: MoralTraceLog(bytes32 logId,int8 currentState,bytes32 decisionId,bytes32 envelopeId,bytes32 justificationObjectHash,bytes32 pillarsCertifiedHash,uint256 committedAt,bytes32 monographVersionHash)
typeHashRegistry.PermissionToken: PermissionToken(bytes32 tokenId,bytes32 logHash,uint256 epochTimestamp,bytes32 signerKeyId,bytes32 merkleRoot,uint256 expiresAt,bytes32 decisionId,bytes32 monographVersionHash)
typeHashRegistry.EmergencyOverride: EmergencyOverride(bytes32 overrideRequestId,uint8 overrideType,bytes32 targetDecisionId,uint8 forcedState,bytes32 justificationHash,uint256 requestedAt,bytes32 monographVersionHash)
typeHashRegistry.CustodianQuorumAttestation: CustodianQuorumAttestation(bytes32 custodianId,bytes32 operationId,uint8 operationType,bytes32 operationPayloadHash,uint256 attestedAt,bytes32 custodianKeyVersion)
typeHashRegistry.TriCameralApproval: TriCameralApproval(bytes32 proposalId,uint8 proposerChamber,bytes32 proposerMemberId,bytes32 proposalHash,bool survivabilityClass,uint256 clockStartTimestamp,uint8 voteNumber,bool vetoExercised,bytes32 monographVersionHash)
typeHashRegistry.GoukassianSignatureAttestation: GoukassianSignatureAttestation(bytes32 signatureId,bytes32 artifactName,bytes32 signerKeyId,bytes32 signedPayloadHash,uint8 signatureAlgorithmId,uint256 signedAt,bytes32 monographVersionHash)

implementationNotes.pqcMigration: SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128s (signatureAlgorithmId 6) and ML-KEM-1024 (signatureAlgorithmId 7) are classified FUTURE per Section 10 (Implementation Gap). Current SHIPPING deployments use ES256 (signatureAlgorithmId 0). PQC algorithm IDs are reserved in this schema to enable forward-compatible migration without a typeHash change.
implementationNotes.noLogNoAction: The PermissionToken.logHash field is the typed-data expression of No Log = No Action. Any off-chain verifier of a signed PermissionToken MUST verify that logHash is included in the corresponding Merkle batch before accepting the token as authorization for actuation.
implementationNotes.sacredZeroEncoding: currentState in MoralTraceLog is typed as int8 to faithfully represent the signed triadic state space (+1, 0, -1). Verifiers must not treat currentState == 0 as false, null, empty, or an error. State 0 is SACRED_ZERO: an active, first-class governance state.
implementationNotes.triCameralGovernance: The TriCameralApproval typed data schema is the EIP-712 expression of the full Tri-Cameral governance architecture. Technical Council (9 members) holds proposal rights only. Stewardship Custodians (11 members) hold binding veto: vetoExercised=true constitutionally blocks any proposal regardless of vote counts. Smart Contract Treasury executes ratification automatically with no admin key. Survivability-class proposals require dual-vote ratification across two 180-day windows per Section 7A. This governance structure is identical to the TL framework by constitutional design.
